close_reason: Already fixed: data.remove() at line 173 and data.into_values() at line 181 both move the ByteString without cloning.
description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:117-118. data is owned (we consumed secret.data at line 114). data.get(k) borrows; bs.0.clone() then clones the Vec. Since we own the BTreeMap, data.remove(k.as_str()) returns Option<ByteString>, and bs.0 (the inner Vec<u8>) can be moved into SecretValue::new with no clone. This avoids one full plaintext-bytes copy on every get. The Vec from k8s-openapi is non-zeroized either way, but a moved Vec lives strictly less time than a cloned Vec.
title: Get path clones the secret bytes when it could move them
